articles.title: Blackout in Cuba leaves millions without power amid US oil chokehold
articles.content:
Latest outage darkens island facing dwindling oil reserves and increasing pressure from Washington

A blackout hit the western half of Cuba on Wednesday, leaving millions of people in Havana and beyond without power in the latest outage to affect an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electricity grid.

The government’s Electric Union confirmed the outage on social platform X, saying it affected people from the eastern town of Pinar del Rio to the central town of Camaguey.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism in wake of deadly speedboat attack
articles.content:
Detainees accused of coming from the US with intent to sow chaos and attack military units on Communist-ruled island

Cuban prosecutors have formally charged six people with crimes of terrorism after a US-flagged speedboat was involved in a deadly shootout with Cuba’s coast guard last week.

The US-based Cuban defendants are accused of packing a boat with weapons and heading toward Cuba in hopes of destabilising the government in Havana.

Continue reading...

articles.title: US military launches operation in Ecuador to combat drug trafficking
articles.content:
US Southern Command said joint mission with Ecuador involves ‘decisive action’ against illicit drug trafficking

US and Ecuadorian forces have launched joint operations to combat drug trafficking, the US Southern Command said on Tuesday, but neither side gave more details.

Southern Command, which encompasses 31 countries through South and Central America and the Caribbean, said in a statement on X that the “decisive action” was aimed at combating illicit drug trafficking.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Donald Trump berates Spain, Friedrich Merz listens
articles.content: German Chancellor Merz's visit with US President Trump inevitably focused on the Iran war. Merz remained silent in the Oval Office as Trump berated Spain, though later the chancellor defended his EU partner.

articles.title: Kim Jong Un inspects new destroyer and pledges naval nuclear expansion
articles.content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un spent two days inspecting his latest 5,000‑ton destroyer and observing cruise missile tests, signalling a major push to expand his navy’s nuclear capabilities. The move comes as Pyongyang continues building additional warships and emphasises maritime power alongside its ballistic missile programme.

articles.title: Brazil's Senate ratifies Mercosur free trade deal with European Union
articles.content: Brazil has formally ratified a landmark free trade deal with the European Union, joining Argentina and Uruguay in creating one of the world’s largest trade blocs. The agreement, decades in the making, removes most tariffs, boosts exports on both sides, and has sparked opposition from European farmers concerned about cheaper imports.

articles.title: 'Notion that the Iranian Kurds could trust anything the Americans said would be pretty reckless'
articles.content: Iranian Kurdish militias have reportedly been consulting with the US in recent days about whether, and ​how, to attack Iranian security forces. A coalition of groups based on the Iran-Iraq border in the semi-autonomous region of Iraqi Kurdistan has been training to mount such an attack in hopes of weakening the regime. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with former ambassador, Peter Galbraith, who previously acted as an advisor to the Kurdistan Regional Government in northern Iraq.

articles.title: Confidence in Gulf countries as destinations and travel hubs 'has been fundamentally broken'
articles.content: Middle East tourism, worth some $367 billion annually to the region, is taking a hit from the widening conflict ​between U.S.-Israel forces and Iran, putting at risk the region's carefully-constructed image as a safe and high-end vacation hotspot after billions in investment in recent years from Abu Dhabi to Dubai. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Anita Mendiratta, travel, aviation and tourism consultant.

articles.title: After US attacks, is Iran's navy on the brink?
articles.content: On Wednesday, American Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters. The Pentagon says one of the main aims of the US-Israeli war against Iran, launched Saturday, is to wipe out the country's navy, and on Wednesday the US claimed to have “effectively neutralised Iran’s major naval presence”. In this edition of Truth or Fake, we look at what we know about the state of the Iranian navy now.

articles.title: Oil prices steady as Iran war continues
articles.content: Oil prices have steadied for the first time since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran. But concerns about energy security and prices are weighing on consumers. In France, long queues formed at petrol stations, despite the Energy Minister's assertion that the public shouldn't worry about shortages. Also in the show - shipping giants Maersk, Cosco, and Hapag-Lloyd suspending all operations in the Gulf, and the Trump administration plans to raise global tariffs to 15% 'sometime this week.'

articles.title: 'Leaving leadership in the hands of temporary council could protect Khamenei successor'
articles.content: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen about the succession plan in Iran, Sahar Razavi, Director of the Iranian and Middle East Studies Centre at California State University, says that "leaving leadership in the hands of a temporary council [...] could protect the potential successor to Khamenei from additional strikes and potential assassination"

articles.title: Iran's wartime succession: What direction after Khamenei?
articles.content: What does succession look like in Iran? The wait continues after last Saturday’s assassination of the Islamic Republic’s supreme leader of nearly 41 years.

articles.title: Death toll of strikes on Iran rises above 1,000, tens of thousands flee Tehran
articles.content: The United Nations on Wednesday said an estimated 100,000 people fled Tehran in the first two days of the US and Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 and March 1. FRANCE 24"s Reza Sayah reports from Iran, where the death toll has climbed over 1,000 people, with "civilians bearing the brunt of this war".

articles.title: 'The fear for the Gulf countries is that the first client of the US would be Israel'
articles.content: "The US is under immense pressure to continue producing as many interceptors as it can, but the fear for the Gulf countries is that the first client of the US would be Israel, rather than the GCC". Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports for FRANCE 24 from Doha in Qatar.

articles.title: Eurozone unemployment unexpectedly falls to record low
articles.content: The eurozone job market showed modest improvement in January 2026, with the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate falling to 6.1%, an all-time low.

articles.title: Latest news bulletin | March 4th, 2026 – Evening
articles.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 4th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

articles.title: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
articles.content: It is the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

articles.title: US Pledges to Maintain Strong Military Force in Korean Peninsula
articles.content: The US military said it remained committed to defending South Korea against the nuclear-armed North after local media reported that Washington was considering redeploying regional US military assets to the Middle East as conflict with Iran spirals.

articles.title: Malaysia to Hold Rate as Mideast War Fuels Fresh Uncertainty
articles.content: Malaysia’s central bank will likely stand pat on borrowing costs, exercising caution as the Iran war unleashes a fresh wave of uncertainty on the global economy.

articles.title: Billionaire Sitting On 16,000 Kilos of Gold Says Price Will Rise
articles.content: Joy Alukkas, an Indian tycoon who built one of the world’s biggest family-owned jewelers, sees gold prices tracking higher over the coming years in response to a world hit by geopolitical and economic risks.

articles.title: South Korean Stocks Lead Asian Rebound, Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Asian equities rebounded, with South Korea leading gains after its worst rout on record, as sentiment showed signs of a recovery following an initial bout of volatility sparked by the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: China Plans $44 Billion Bonds to Boost Capital at Top Banks
articles.content: China will issue special sovereign bonds to recapitalize some of its largest banks, marking an expansion of Beijing’s efforts to fortify the nation’s $69 trillion financial system against a cooling economy and market volatility.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs AM Says Non-Payment in Private Credit Remains Low
articles.content: Goldman Sachs Asset Management LP Global Co-Head of Private Credit James Reynolds said the private credit sector remains resilient, with few borrowers struggling to meet loan payments.

articles.title: China Vows to Tackle Property Crunch With City-Specific Policies
articles.content: Chinese officials pledged to stabilize the real estate sector at a crucial political meeting, but stopped short of announcing major policies some economists think are needed to turn around a yearslong slump.

articles.title: Medline’s Buyout Firm Backers Raise $3.1 Billion in Share Sale
articles.content: Some of Medline Inc.’s biggest shareholders raised $3.1 billion in a share sale, capitalizing on the medical supplier’s strong trading since its initial public offering in December.

articles.title: Anthropic chief back in talks with Pentagon about AI deal
articles.content: Dario Amodei is meeting deputy to Pete Hegseth to reach a compromise on military use of the technology

articles.title: OpenAI pushes to add surveillance safeguards following Pentagon deal
articles.content: Sam Altman’s start-up planning additional protections as it implements the agreement hastily announced on Friday

articles.title: South Korea's Kospi stages a sharp rebound, surging over 12%, on course for best day on record
articles.content: Asia-Pacific markets opened higher Thursday, rebounding after several days of steep losses.

articles.title: China to reportedly boost defense spending by 7%, slowest pace since 2021
articles.content: Beijing's planned military budget, reported by Reuters, comes amid increased conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: The U.S. insists the Iran conflict won't be a 'forever war.' Experts beg to differ
articles.content: President Trump and his officials say the war won't be protracted. Experts say it could go on longer than the White House expects.

articles.title: Broadcom's custom AI chip business stays hot and gives the bulls a much-needed win
articles.content: Shares of Broadcom rose about 5% in extended trading after its earnings report underscored the strength of its custom chip business.

articles.title: Prices to charter large oil tankers soar as Strait of Hormuz traffic grinds to a halt
articles.content: Charter rates nearly doubled in recent days and are up fivefold since the start of the year.

articles.title: Demonstrators across Iran condemn US-Israeli war
articles.content: Iranian media shared footage from several cities showing demonstrators condemning the US-Israeli war.

articles.title: Senate Republicans block war powers resolution on Iran
articles.content: Senate Republicans, joined by a Democrat, voted down a war powers resolution on President Donald Trump's war with Iran.

articles.title: Trump had a ‘good feeling’ Iran would strike the US: White House
articles.content: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said US President Donald Trump had a “good feeling” Iran was about to attac

articles.title: Korean Stocks Surge by Most Since 2008 in Roller Coaster Week
articles.content: South Korean equities bounced back after their biggest one-day slump on record, as bargain hunters returned to a market battered by panic selling.

articles.title: What Does China's GDP Target Reveal?
articles.content: Xiaojia Zhi, Credit Agricole Chief China economist, tells Bloomberg TV that China's modest GDP growth targets reflects a desire to boost domestic demand while limiting fiscal support to those sectors where it is most urgently required. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Fear Index Shows Traders Bracing For More Selling in Indian Shares As War Rages
articles.content: Traders are buying protection against fresh uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Philippine Inflation Hits One-Year High, May Curb Further Easing
articles.content: The Philippines’ inflation rate rose to the fastest pace in over a year in February, putting the Southeast Asian economy in a delicate position ahead of the expected surge in prices of goods in the wake of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Carney Won’t Rule Out Military Action If Mideast Conflict Widens
articles.content: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said “one can never categorically rule out” participation in military action resulting from the war in Iran, and the country will stand by its allies if necessary.

articles.title: Iron Ore Rises as Beijing Vows Measures to Support Steel Sector
articles.content: Iron ore rose as China renewed a pledge to tackle overcapacity in its world-leading steel sector and set a widely anticipated economic growth target for the year ahead.

articles.title: China’s Steady Debt Plan Calms Bond Market on Ample Liquidity
articles.content: China’s 2026 debt issuance target is aligning with market expectations, easing concerns among bond traders who are supported by ample liquidity and growing bets on policy easing.

articles.title: Deflation and tariff-hit China sets lowest annual growth target on record at 4.5% to 5%
articles.content: That marks a slight downgrade from the "around 5%" target set in the past three years.

articles.title: South Korea's Kospi stages a sharp rebound, surging over 10%, on course for best day since 2008
articles.content: Asia-Pacific markets opened higher Thursday, rebounding after several days of steep losses.

articles.title: China to boost defense spending by 7%, slowest pace since 2021
articles.content: Beijing's planned military budget comes amid escalating conflict in the Middle East and persistent tensions over Taiwan.
